member.name: Mr Gregory Campbell
member.synopsis: Mr Gregory Campbell is the Democratic Unionist Party MP for East Londonderry , and has been an MP continually since 7 June 2001.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Knowledge Transfer Partnerships
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, whether she plans to expand the Knowledge Transfer Partnership scheme in this Parliament.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is committed to supporting innovation and economic growth through programmes that strengthen collaboration between businesses and universities, including the Knowledge Transfer Partnership (KTP) programme. Opportunities for KTPs are open fo...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Homicide and Rape: Convictions
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, how many convictions of (a) murder, (b) attempted murder and (c) rape were (i) increased and (ii) decreased under the Unduly Lenient Sentence scheme in the last five years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The number of convictions for murder, attempted murder and rape for which the Court of Appeal increased a sentence under the Unduly Lenient Sentence scheme in the last five years is set out below. No sentences for murder, attempted murder and rape were de...

writtenQuestions.heading: Aviation: Alternative Fuels
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, what assessment her Department has made of the adequacy of progress towards meeting the target of 10% of UK aviation fuel required to be sustainable aviation fuel by 2030.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The latest SAF Mandate 2025 provisional statistical release was published on 13 May 2026. These provisional statistics show that, in 2025, over 2% of all reported aviation fuel supply was verified and unverified SAF. Whilst these statistics are provisiona...

writtenQuestions.heading: Cybersecurity: Business
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what steps the National Cyber Security Centre is taking to reduce the (a) number and (b) severity of cyber attacks on UK businesses.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The National Cyber Security Centre works round the clock to reduce both the number and severity of cyber attacks affecting UK businesses through a combination of protective cyber defence interventions, practical guidance, incident management and cyber res...

writtenQuestions.heading: Global Combat Air Programme: Contracts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Defence, what progress has been made regarding an International contract between the GCAP International Government Organisation and Edgewing.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The GCAP International Government Organisation signed an international contract with Edgewing on 3 July 2026 to progress the development and delivery of a next-generation combat aircraft.

writtenQuestions.heading: Charities: Assets
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, pursuant to the answer of 2 July 2026 to WPQ 13776, whether any disposal assets were recovered from those four charities.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Charitable funds were recovered from three of the charities and were transferred to another charity or charities with similar objects (as required by the Charities Act 2011). One charity had no remaining assets that could be transferred.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Buses: Electric Vehicles
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Transport, whether the zero emission bus order pipeline will be reviewed and updated on an annual basis throughout the ten year period.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The zero emission bus order pipeline was published to provide greater long‑term certainty to the sector by setting out anticipated demand for new buses over a ten‑year period.Through the UK Bus Manufacturing Expert Panel, partners have committed to keepin...

writtenQuestions.heading: Individual Savings Accounts
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, of people holding cash ISAs in 2024/2025, what estimate she has made of the proportion of the total number were in the £10,000 - £50,000 per annum salary range.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In the latest tax year available, 2022 to 2023, 71.5% of individuals holding a cash ISA had an income in the range of £10,000 to £49,999.

writtenQuestions.heading: Stocks and Shares: USA
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Chancellor of the Exchequer, pursuant to the Answer of 1 July to Question 13470, what guidance she has given the Listings Taskforce on (a) an outcome of their deliberations and (b) reporting their findings.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the honorable member to my responses to UIN 10566 and 13470.

writtenQuestions.heading: Home Shopping: Northern Ireland
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Northern Ireland, what discussions he has had with Cabinet colleagues since January 2026 on ensuring that Northern Ireland consumers are not disadvantaged regarding online sales from GB based businesses.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I have regular discussions with Cabinet colleagues on a range of Northern Ireland related matters, including Northern Ireland’s place within the UK internal market. The Windsor Framework’s UK Carrier (UKC) Scheme allows GB based businesses to work with pa...

writtenQuestions.heading: Retail Trade: Urban Areas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Housing, Communities and Local Government, if he will hold discussions with the devolved institutions and elected mayors on a coordinated approach to help tackle the problems faced by High Streets.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Ministers regularly engage with elected mayors on matters relating to local growth and regeneration and remain committed to collaborating with the Devolved Governments on shared challenges.Through our devolution agenda, we are empowering local leaders wit...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Ministry of Housing, Communities and Local Government

writtenQuestions.heading: Broadband: Social Tariffs
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Science, Innovation and Technology, what estimate she has made of the change in public awareness of, and take-up of, broadband and mobile social tariffs between December 2023 and December 2025.
writtenQuestions.answerText: There are around 30 social tariff offers currently available to eligible consumers, however, Ofcom data shows that the take up remains relatively low: increasing from 380,000 households in September 2023 to 532,000 by June 2025. Due to an expansion in the...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Science, Innovation and Technology

writtenQuestions.heading: Parliamentary Estate: Concrete
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the hon. Member for Blaenau Gwent and Rhymney, representing the House of Commons Commission, how many areas in the Parliamentary estate where reinforced autoclaved aerated concrete has been identified that have yet to be repaired.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Reinforced autoclaved aerated concrete (RAAC) has been identified in locations on the third-floor Upper Committee Corridor North and South, and in the section of T-Block adjacent to the toilets on the first to third floors. Repair solutions are being deve...

writtenQuestions.heading: Iron and Steel: Import Duties
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, if he will help ensure that Departmental officials undertake to meet with Members from Northern Ireland following requests for such a meeting regarding the steel tariffs issue.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Yes.

writtenQuestions.heading: Loneliness: Mental Health
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what assessment she has made of links between loneliness and mental health issues, particularly among younger people aged between 16 - 35.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Loneliness can be experienced by anyone at any time, however we know that certain groups are at higher risk, including those aged 16-34. Research also highlights that the relationship between loneliness and mental health issues can be bi-directional, with...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Access to Work Programme
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Work and Pensions, whether he plans to announce any changes to Access to Work by the end of 2026.
writtenQuestions.answerText: We have already taken steps to improve the Access to Work scheme, committing to recruiting and training nearly 500 additional staff. Recruitment has already started. New case managers will receive extensive training to handle complex applications with con...

writtenQuestions.heading: Deep Vein Thrombosis
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of trends in the number of people hospitalised by deep vein thrombosis in the last 10 years.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Department has not made a specific assessment. The following table shows a count of Finished Admission Episodes in hospitals in England where there was a primary diagnosis, or any diagnosis, of 'deep vein thrombosis' for activity between 2015/16 and 2...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Charities: Registration
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, on how many occasions between 2020 and 2025 did the Charity Commission withdraw charitable status from previoulsy registered charities, following investigations into those same groups.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Between 2020 and 2025, the Charity Commission has made orders for 4 charities to be wound up under s84B of the Charities Act 2011. It found in all cases that there had been misconduct or mismanagement in the administration of the charity, that making the ...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport

writtenQuestions.heading: Dental Services
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Health and Social Care, what assessment he has made of the time in an average week NHS dentists have spent carrying out private dental work between 2020 and 2025.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Data is not held on the average amount of time National Health Service dentists spent carrying out private dental work for each year between 2020 and 2025.
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department of Health and Social Care

writtenQuestions.heading: Musicians: EU Countries
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Culture, Media and Sport, what steps she is taking to improve UK-EU cultural cooperation, particularly for UK based musicians wanting to perform in European destinations.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As noted in the Common Understanding from the inaugural UK-EU Summit in May last year, the UK and EU are committed to cultural and artistic exchange, including through the activities of touring artists. This is a priority, as set out in the Government’s m...
writtenQuestions.answeringBody: Department for Culture, Media and Sport
